item.title: Trump signals flexibility on Iran nuclear enrichment, open to 20-year suspension
item.description: President Trump indicated he would accept Iran suspending nuclear enrichment for 20 years if there is a real guarantee, marking a shift from his previous demands for total cessation.

item.title: US military says 78 ships redirected from Strait of Hormuz amid Iran blockade
item.description: CENTCOM reports that 78 commercial vessels have been redirected and 4 disabled to ensure compliance with the Hormuz closure, underscoring the escalating disruption to global shipping.

item.title: Italy's Meloni wins European backing for third-country migrant return hubs
item.description: Italian PM Giorgia Meloni hailed a shift in European migration policy as her controversial approach to offshore migrant processing gains support across the Council of Europe.

item.title: France to develop ballistic missile defense system with Ukraine
item.description: President Zelensky announced France's readiness to cooperate on ballistic missile defense, calling it an important step in strengthening Ukraine's ability to repel Russian attacks.

item.title: Poll: Russians fear strikes at home more than front line for first time since 2022
item.description: Ukrainian drone strikes deep inside Russia have shifted Russian public concern from battlefield developments to domestic security, according to a new poll.

item.title: Putin to Meet Xi in Beijing May 19-20 Following Trump Summit
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin will visit China for two days of talks with Xi Jinping on May 19-20, discussing bilateral relations and international issues just days after Trump concluded his own summit in Beijing.

item.title: UAE States All Actions Were Defensive Measures to Protect Sovereignty
item.description: The United Arab Emirates foreign ministry issued a statement saying all actions taken by the Gulf country were defensive measures aimed at protecting its sovereignty, civilians, and vital infrastructure amid regional tensions.

item.title: FIFA Officials to Meet Iranian FA Over World Cup Participation
item.description: FIFA Secretary-General Mattias Grafstrom will meet Iranian Football Federation officials in Istanbul to offer reassurance over Iran participation in the upcoming World Cup amid the ongoing Iran-US conflict.

item.title: Eurovision Song Contest Final Proceeds Amid Gaza Boycott
item.description: Five countries have withdrawn from the Eurovision Song Contest final over Israel participation, with the event taking place against the backdrop of ongoing Middle East tensions.

item.title: US Reportedly Preparing to Indict Former Cuban Leader Raul Castro
item.description: The US Justice Department is reportedly preparing to criminally indict former Cuban leader Raul Castro in the coming days, marking a significant development in US-Cuba relations.

item.title: Congo Confirms New Ebola Outbreak with 80 Deaths
item.description: Africa CDC confirms Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo's Ituri province. Health officials are mobilizing response efforts to contain the spread.

item.title: US Proposes Dramatic Colorado River Water Cuts Amid Drought
item.description: US government plans could cut up to 40% of water supplies to Arizona, California and Nevada. The drought-stricken Colorado River basin faces unprecedented pressure from climate change and overuse.

item.title: Trump-Targeted Senator Cassidy Faces Louisiana Primary Test
item.description: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy, targeted by Trump for retribution over impeachment vote, faces competitive primary in Louisiana. Results will test Trump's influence on GOP congressional races.

item.title: OpenAI Signs Deal to Provide ChatGPT Plus to All Maltese Citizens
item.description: OpenAI partners with Malta's government to give all residents access to ChatGPT Plus for one year following AI training course. First nationwide AI access program of its kind.

item.title: Trump's Geopolitical Brinkmanship Hits Wall With Iran
item.description: After a year of concessions on tariffs and conflicts, U.S. President Donald Trump's confrontational negotiating style has met its match in Iran, where neither threats nor military action have produced the desired outcome.

item.title: Trump Returns From China With Stability and Stalemate
item.description: President Donald Trump's Beijing summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping produced modest results but highlighted China's desire for stability after last year's trade war extremes, with the US-China relationship now defined by managed competition.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon Extend Ceasefire by 45 Days
item.description: Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend their ceasefire by 45 days to enable further diplomatic progress, the U.S. State Department announced, even as Israel continues strikes it says fall outside the truce agreement.

item.title: China Confirms Boeing Aircraft Purchase Deal Following Trump Visit
item.description: Chinese Ministry of Commerce confirms Beijing will buy at least 200 Boeing aircraft and engines, marking a significant trade concession following Trump's summit with Xi.

item.title: Pakistan Interior Minister Arrives in Tehran for Talks With Iranian Officials
item.description: Pakistan's Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi visits Tehran amid regional tensions, as Pakistan navigates relations with Iran during the US-Iran conflict.

item.title: Putin to Visit Beijing Days After Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin will meet with Xi Jinping on a two-day trip to Beijing next week, signaling continued Russia-China coordination following Trump's visit.

item.title: Moscow Signals Frustration as US-Russia Contacts Remain Difficult
item.description: Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Ryabkov indicates that US-Russia diplomatic contacts remain slow and difficult amid ongoing tensions.

item.title: ISIS Deputy Leader Killed in Joint US-Nigeria Operation
item.description: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, the second-in-command of ISIS globally, was killed in a joint operation by US and Nigerian forces in northeastern Nigeria. Both presidents Trump and Tinubu confirmed the elimination of the terrorist leader.

item.title: Zelenskiy Warns Russia May Attack NATO From Belarus
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy stated that Russia is considering plans to attack Ukraine's north or a NATO country from Belarusian territory, drawing Belarus deeper into the war.

item.title: US Accuses Iraqi Man of Helping Iran-Backed Militia Plot Attacks in US and Europe
item.description: The US Justice Department announced the arrest of an Iraqi national accused of involvement in multiple attacks against US interests in Europe and planning attacks on US soil for an Iran-backed militia.

item.title: Canada Deepens Arctic Defense Ties With Nordic Countries After Trump Greenland Threats
item.description: Following Trump's threats to seize Greenland, Canadian authorities have been strengthening defense cooperation with Nordic countries. Greenland has been seeking support from its northern ally Canada.
